free_text: University of Hull, HU6 7RX, United Kingdom .
- Li target was produced by evaporation 30 um
of Li onto a water-cooled copper backing;
- Oxygen target consisted of a 0.2-mm-thick tantalum
disk with an oxide layer ~2 um in thickness, which was
produced by electrolysis of water with 95% enrichment
Proton beam current typically 100 uA in all
Quasistellar neutron spectra at kT = 5.1 and 25 keV,
Samples were prepared from NaCl (99.99% pure) pressed
into cylindrical pellets 6, 8, 10, 15 mm in diameter
Water content was checked by heating a quantity of the
NaCl at 250 degC for 2 hr, showing that the sample mass
before and after heating differed by less than 0.01%.
Uncertainties of Na samples mass less than 0.05 mg.
Gold foils of 0.03mm thickness were fixed to the front
and back of the samples during irradiation.

Li glass detector for neutron flux of kT=25keV
measurement; placed at 831 mm from the target.
High purity Ge detector of 39 cm3 - to measure
Two HPGe clover detectors - to measure the induced
exp.spectra were calculated using the evaluate CS
